title: Pycreus longistolon
taxonomy: Pycreus longistolon is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a member of the genus Pycreus, which is a group of sedges that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Pycreus longistolon is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cyperoideae. The family Cyperaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots that includes many species of sedges and rushes. Pycreus longistolon is classified as a plant species within the kingdom Plantae.

Its roots and rhizomes are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. intro: Pycreus longistolon, also known as long-stolonated sedge, is a plant species belonging to the Cyperaceae family, characterized by its distinctive sedge-like appearance and long stolons. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues.
